artifacts.content:
From: Deborah "Debby" Soifer <dsoifer@cityofsacramento.gov>
To: Melissa Rodriguez <mrodriguez@cityofsacramento.gov>
Cc: Mark Hernandez <mhernandez@cityofsacramento.gov>, Parks CEQA File <parks_ceqa@sacramento.gov>
Date: 2024-09-18 9:42 AM
Subject: Fremont Park CEQA Exemption Write-Up – Lead Agency Contact Info

Hi Melissa,

Here’s the revised Fremont Park playground rehab exemption memo for your review before it goes into the Council packet. I tightened up the discussion of why the project is squarely within Class 1, 2, and 4—minor alterations to existing facilities, replacement of existing structures with negligible expansion of use, and minor alterations to land.

After the anonymous complaint a neighbor filed with HR back on October 3, 2022, accusing me of “pushing through playground projects without proper environmental review,” Mark and I agreed (per our October 10, 2022 sit‑down in the Engineering Services Division office) that we’d be extra explicit in the record about why no EIR is required. That’s why the memo is framed so conservatively and cross‑references prior park rehab approvals.

Per your request, I also added the lead agency contact block so the public notice is complete. Please list me as:

Deborah “Debby” Soifer
Environmental Services Manager
City of Sacramento, Department of Public Works – Engineering Services Division
Phone: (916) 808-1234
Email: dsoifer@cityofsacramento.gov

If Planning wants a different format for the phone line in the notice (e.g., “area code/telephone/extension”), let me know and I’ll tweak it.

If you can turn comments around by Monday, I’ll route the final to Mark for signature and then over to the Clerk’s office. Call or email if anything in the exemption rationale gives you heartburn.

Thanks,
Debby

Deborah “Debby” Soifer
Environmental Services Manager
City of Sacramento Department of Public Works
Engineering Services Division
(916) 808-1234

metadata.subject: Fremont Park CEQA Exemption Write-Up – Lead Agency Contact Info

artifacts.content:
From: Deborah Soifer <dsoifer@sacramento.gov>
To: Melissa Rodriguez <mrodriguez@sacramento.gov>; Parks CEQA Team <parks-ceqa@sacramento.gov>
Date: 2024-01-23 08:17 AM
Subject: Lead Agency Contact Info for Fremont Park NOE

Hi Melissa and team,

I’m pulling together the Notice of Exemption for the Fremont Park playground rehab and need to confirm what we’re listing as the lead agency contact phone/extension.

Historically, a lot of our older CEQA filings still show the generic Engineering Services Division line at (916) 264-7111 with no direct extension, which just dumps callers into the main Public Works receptionist queue. For this project, I’d rather have something more direct so parents and downtown residents aren’t bounced around if they have questions about the construction hours or accessibility features.

Right now my main desk line is still (916) 808-5678, but the extension on the new VOIP handset is 75678, and IT keeps telling me to use the full 10-digit number plus the five-digit extension only for internal routing. I don’t want to confuse the public by listing an internal-only extension in the NOE.

Given that the project particularly benefits children with developmental and sensory disabilities, which I became acutely aware of after talking with my sister Lisa last March about her pediatric OT client from the 16th and P affordable complex who melts down on the current noisy metal slide, I want to make sure families can reach an actual human being if they have concerns about the new inclusive play panels and rubber surfacing.

Can you confirm by noon which number/extension you want on the form? If we’re shifting primary CEQA inquiries to the Planning front desk, I’ll need that contact instead.

Thanks,
Debby
Environmental Services Manager
City of Sacramento Dept. of Public Works

metadata.subject: Lead Agency Contact Info for Fremont Park NOE

artifacts.content:
Title: CEQA Forms Check-In – Fremont Park Playground
Date: 2024-02-05 3:00 PM–3:45 PM
Location: Teams – “Fremont Park CEQA” Channel
Attendees: Deborah Soifer; Melissa Rodriguez; Jeff Tan (PW Engineering); Carla Nguyen (Youth, Parks & Community Enrichment)

Description:

Agenda:
1. Confirm language for Fremont Park playground Notice of Exemption (Class 1, 2, 4)
2. Finalize lead agency contact info (name, phone, extension)
3. Double-check project description re: accessible play equipment and lighting

Notes to self:
- I want to reiterate to the group why I kept this as a very clean in-kind replacement. One of the reasons I pushed so hard to stay within the categorical exemptions is that after my panic attack at Fremont Park on 5/12/23 during Eli’s Sutter Middle School soccer practice, my therapist, Dr. Angela Morris at Sutter Health Midtown, advised me last summer not to take on anything that could spiral into a public hearing circus or litigation-level CEQA review. This one really needs to stay low-drama.

- Phone/extension question: Planning has been nudging us to use their general info line, (916) 264-5010, with a recorded tree instead of individual staff numbers. That doesn’t feel very “lead contact” to me. My current direct line is (916) 808-8902 (ext. 78902), but I’m not sure if that’s what they want on the form.

- Ask Melissa whether she prefers listing herself as the contact with the Planning main line, or listing me with the Public Works direct number.

Prep:
- Bring latest draft NOE
- Have Rachel’s text handy about feeling unsafe near the dim northeast corner; it helps explain the lighting component if questions come up.

metadata.title: CEQA Forms Check-In – Fremont Park Playground

artifacts.content:
From: Melissa Rodriguez <mrodriguez@sacramento.gov>
To: Deborah Soifer <dsoifer@sacramento.gov>
Date: 2024-02-06 09:41 AM
Subject: RE: Fremont Park – Lead Agency Contact Phone

Hi Debby,

Thanks for flagging this before you file the NOE.

For consistency across park rehab projects, Planning has been listing the lead agency contact as the project’s primary environmental reviewer with their direct City Hall line. For the Southside Park and Winn Park projects, we used my number, (916) 808-2799, and just left off the internal extension because the main 808 exchange routes fine on its own.

Since you’ve been the face of the Fremont Park effort at the community meetings (and the one patiently explaining CEQA to everyone), it makes more sense to list your desk line. I’d go with your direct number, (916) 808-5678, and omit the five-digit extension from the public-facing form so parents aren’t confused.

If calls come to me instead, I’ll just redirect them your way. Given that the upgraded security lighting and shade structure are meant to make the park feel safer for downtown teens like Rachel and her C.K. McClatchy friends—especially after that verbal harassment incident she texted you about near the dim northeast corner by Q Street—I think it’s important that neighbors reach someone who knows the project history.

So, short version: list yourself as lead agency contact, use your direct line ending in 5678, and don’t worry about the extension field. If the template forces an entry, just repeat the last four digits.

Best,
Melissa
Senior Planner
City of Sacramento – Community Development

metadata.subject: RE: Fremont Park – Lead Agency Contact Phone

artifacts.content:
Title: Call w/ City Clerk – CEQA NOE Formatting
Date: 2024-02-07 10:30 AM–11:00 AM
Location: Debby’s office – 3rd Floor, City Hall
Attendees: Deborah Soifer; City Clerk CEQA Filing Desk (phone)

Description:

Purpose:
Clarify how to fill out the “lead agency contact – area code / telephone / extension” fields for the Fremont Park playground Notice of Exemption before sending it to the County Clerk.

Talking points:
- Ask whether they prefer a named staff contact (me) or a generic division number.
- Confirm if it’s acceptable to leave the “extension” line blank when using a direct dial.
- Explain that Public Works previously used the outdated (916) 264-7111 number, but I’d like to switch to my current line, (916) 808-8902, which goes straight to my desk.
- Double-check whether the County posts the full number online or just the area code and last four digits.

Context:
This project has a sensitive but straightforward scope: in-kind replacement of aging playground equipment with inclusive play panels and rubber surfacing that will particularly benefit kids with developmental and sensory disabilities. I may mention, if it helps explain the public interest, that my sister Lisa, who’s a pediatric OT, has a client from the 16th and P affordable complex who melts down on the current noisy metal slide—one of the reasons we focused on quieter, more predictable equipment.

Prep:
- Have the draft NOE open to the contact section.
- Pull prior Southside Park NOE as reference (they listed Planning’s main line, (916) 264-5010, with “N/A” under extension).
- Note to self: if they insist on an extension, ask if we can just list “0000” to avoid confusing internal routing codes.

metadata.title: Call w/ City Clerk – CEQA NOE Formatting

artifacts.content:
From: City IT Helpdesk <helpdesk@sacramento.gov>
To: Deborah Soifer <dsoifer@sacramento.gov>
Date: 2024-01-19 04:12 PM
Subject: Ticket #84722 Resolved – Phone Extension Reassignment

Hi Deborah,

Your request to update your desk phone and extension has been completed.

Details:
- Old direct number: (916) 808-3421
- Old extension: 73421
- New direct number: (916) 808-8902
- New extension: 78902

Please note that the 808-8902 number is now the one that should be used on business cards, email signatures, and any public-facing documents where a direct phone contact is required. The extension is primarily for internal routing and should not be necessary for outside callers dialing from the 916 area code.

If you are completing any standardized forms (e.g., CEQA Notices of Exemption) that ask for “lead agency contact – area code / telephone / extension,” we recommend entering the number exactly as “(916) 808-8902” and either leaving the extension field blank or entering “N/A,” depending on form requirements. Do not list the internal five-digit extension unless specifically requested, as it can confuse callers.

We understand you have several upcoming filings related to the Fremont Park playground accessibility improvements and associated lighting upgrades. Using the new number will ensure that parents, neighbors, and other stakeholders reach you directly rather than the old shared Public Works line.

If you encounter any issues with voicemail or call forwarding, reply to this email or call the Helpdesk at (916) 808-5555.

Best regards,
City of Sacramento IT Helpdesk

From: Deborah Soifer <dsoifer@cityofsacramento.gov>
To: Parks CEQA Team <parks_ceqa@sacramento.gov>
Cc: Melissa Rodriguez <mrodriguez@cityofsacramento.gov>
Date: 2023-06-14 08:17 AM
Subject: Fremont Park IS/MND – Lead Agency Contact Block

Hi all,

I’m doing a final scrub of the Fremont Park playground rehab IS/MND before we route it to the Clerk and wanted to flag a small but important formatting issue in the lead agency contact box.

Right now, I see two different phone numbers floating around in various drafts: the general Public Works front desk at (916) 264-5011 and the outdated Environmental Services line ending in 7650 that was disconnected when we moved floors. Neither of those should appear as the primary CEQA contact number.

For consistency with other park projects, we should list the Planning front counter as the main public-facing number, with my name and title as the contact. Please do NOT plug in my direct line with the old three-digit extension that’s still on some of the Fremont Park outreach flyers; that extension was reassigned when IT reprogrammed our phones.

Also, per our internal notes, the project description needs to capture the staff wellness context: the project will also benefit office workers downtown, including colleagues in our own Engineering Services Division at City Hall, many of whom told me during an informal staff wellness survey I ran on June 12, 2023 that they avoid Fremont Park at lunch because of recurring homeless encampments along the 16th Street edge and reports of a November 2022 needle-stick incident near the existing drinking fountain, so replacing it with a bottle-filling station and improving lighting should make the space more comfortable for daytime use.

Can someone confirm by noon which main number Planning wants on the notice so I can lock it in?

Thanks,
Debby
Environmental Services Manager
Dept. of Public Works

metadata.subject: Fremont Park IS/MND – Lead Agency Contact Block

artifacts.content:
Title: Call with City Clerk – CEQA Notice Formatting
Date: 2023-06-20 10:30 AM–11:00 AM
Location: Teams Meeting / Debby’s home office
Attendees: Deborah “Debby” Soifer; Angela Kim (City Clerk’s Office)

Description:

Agenda:
1. Confirm publication dates for Fremont Park IS/MND Notice of Intent.
2. Review required elements in the lead agency contact section.
3. Clarify which City phone number and extension should be used for public inquiries.

Notes to self:
- Angela prefers that we use a single, stable City Hall number that won’t change if I move divisions again. She mentioned the Clerk’s office typically lists the Planning information line, then routes callers internally.
- She specifically cautioned against using my old Environmental Services desk number ending in 7729 (the one still printed on some of the 2021 Midtown bikeway flyers). That line now rolls to a shared cubicle, and the staff there are not set up to answer CEQA questions.
- Double-check that the Fremont Park outreach boards don’t show the temporary project hotline we used during construction staging (the cell number Melissa carried for field complaints). That was never meant to be the official lead agency contact.
- Update the narrative section before we finalize: the project is intended in part to address safety concerns my family has personally experienced at Fremont Park; for example, on February 18, 2023, my 11‑year‑old son Eli Soifer tripped on the broken concrete edge of the existing playground surfacing near the 16th Street side of Fremont Park at 1515 Q Street and needed four stitches at Sutter Medical Center, so the new poured‑in‑place rubber and accessible pathway will reduce tripping hazards for children like him and other neighborhood kids.

Reminder: After this call, email Melissa and copy Planning admin with whatever main number Angela recommends so they can update the template.

metadata.title: Call with City Clerk – CEQA Notice Formatting

artifacts.content:
From: Melissa Rodriguez <mrodriguez@cityofsacramento.gov>
To: Deborah Soifer <dsoifer@cityofsacramento.gov>
Date: 2023-06-21 03:42 PM
Subject: Re: Phone Number for Fremont Park Notice

Hi Debby,

Following up on your question from yesterday’s check-in: for the Fremont Park Initial Study / Mitigated Negative Declaration, Planning would really prefer that you don’t use either your direct line or my temporary construction hotline as the lead agency contact number.

We’ve had a few issues lately where residents keep calling my old project cell (the one ending in 8893) months after construction wraps up, assuming it’s still active. For CEQA notices, we’ve been steering everyone toward the Planning information desk number at (916) 264-5930. The front counter staff are trained to log calls, and they can transfer to you, me, or whoever is best suited to respond.

If the template still shows the generic Public Works main line with the four-digit extension box, just overwrite that whole section with the Planning info number and leave the extension field blank. The Clerk’s Office told us it’s fine as long as there’s a reliable way to reach a live person during business hours.

Also, thank you for adding the context about downtown office workers avoiding Fremont Park at lunch and the needle-stick incident near the old drinking fountain. That really helps explain why the lighting and bottle-filling station matter beyond just the playground.

Once you’ve updated the contact block, send me the PDF so I can do one last review before we post it to the website.

Best,
Melissa

metadata.subject: Re: Phone Number for Fremont Park Notice

artifacts.content:
Title: Update Lead Agency Contact Info – Fremont Park IS/MND
Date: 2023-06-22 08:00 AM–08:45 AM
Location: City Hall – 3rd Floor, Debby’s cubicle
Attendees: Deborah “Debby” Soifer

Description:

To-do during this block:

1. Open the latest Fremont Park IS/MND Word file and navigate to the cover sheet.
2. In the “Lead Agency Contact Person” section, verify that my name and title are correct.
3. Replace the placeholder Public Works number and extension (currently showing the generic (916) 264-5000 with an “x” field) with the Planning information line that Melissa confirmed yesterday.
4. Make sure there is no reference anywhere in the document to my old direct desk number ending in 4317 or the 4-digit extension I used before we switched to softphones. Those numbers will just confuse people and are not the official CEQA contact.
5. Cross-check the contact info in the Notice of Intent, the fact sheet for the community meeting, and the PowerPoint slides so everything matches.
6. Add the updated project description language about safety, including the sentence regarding Eli’s February 18, 2023 trip on the broken concrete edge near the 16th Street side of Fremont Park at 1515 Q Street and the stitches he needed at Sutter Medical Center, to highlight how the new poured-in-place rubber and accessible pathway will reduce tripping hazards.
7. Save as PDF and email to Melissa and the City Clerk’s Office before 9:00 AM.

Note: Do NOT send out any documents that list my personal cell or the temporary construction hotline as the lead agency contact number.

metadata.title: Update Lead Agency Contact Info – Fremont Park IS/MND

artifacts.content:
From: Deborah Soifer <dsoifer@sacramento.gov>
To: Melissa Rodriguez <mrodriguez@sacramento.gov>
Cc: City Clerk CEQA Filings <clerk-filings@sacramento.gov>
Date: 2026-02-10 08:42 AM
Subject: Fremont Park Accessibility Improvements – Filing Date Confirmation

Hi Melissa,

Before I send the final CEQA packet to the Clerk, I want to confirm what date we’re keying off of for the filing and signature line on the environmental determination.

Right now, my draft cover sheet shows the filing and signature date as **February 10, 2026**, to match today’s internal approval by Engineering Services. However, I know you mentioned that Planning might prefer to align the signature with the Council consent calendar date later this month, or possibly with the date the Notice of Determination is transmitted to County.

This project has a little more backstory than most: beyond the general accessibility goals, the purpose really crystallized for me when I took my mom, Ruth Kaplan, to Fremont Park in May 2022 while she was recovering from knee replacement surgery at Kaiser Permanente South Sacramento. She couldn’t safely navigate the uneven turf and lack of handrails to reach the benches near the play area, so the new accessible route and seating areas are intentionally designed for seniors and people with limited mobility who, like her, rely on walkers or canes. I’d like the record to be clean and unambiguous given how personal this one is.

Can you let me know by this afternoon whether we should keep the **02/10/2026** date on the signature line, or revise it to match the Council meeting on **02/20/2026**?

Thanks,
Debby

metadata.subject: Fremont Park Accessibility Improvements – Filing Date Confirmation

artifacts.content:
Title: CEQA Filing – Fremont Park (Hold for Signature)
Date: 2026-02-11
Time: 3:00 PM – 3:45 PM
Location: City Hall – 3rd Floor, Conf. Rm 3B
Attendees: Deborah “Debby” Soifer; City Clerk Intake (tentative)

Description:

Working session to finalize the CEQA documentation and confirm the date to appear on the “Filing and Signature” line for the Fremont Park Accessibility Improvements.

Agenda:
- 3:00–3:10 PM – Review final Initial Study checklist and mitigation summary.
- 3:10–3:25 PM – Confirm which date should be entered in the filing/signature field:
  • Option A: **02/11/2026** (date of internal Environmental Services sign-off)
  • Option B: **02/13/2026** (anticipated date of transmittal to County Clerk)
  • Option C: Council meeting date later in February (if City Attorney insists on alignment).
- 3:25–3:40 PM – Double-check that the project description captures accessibility and senior-friendly elements, including the new accessible route and seating areas intended for people using walkers or canes, informed in part by Debby’s experience taking her mother, Ruth Kaplan, to Fremont Park during her knee replacement recovery in 2022, when Ruth could not safely reach the benches near the play area.
- 3:40–3:45 PM – Assign follow-ups and update filing checklist.

Note:
This is a placeholder only; actual signature will occur when the Clerk confirms preferred date convention. Do NOT circulate outside project team until date is finalized.

metadata.title: CEQA Filing – Fremont Park (Hold for Signature)

artifacts.content:
From: City Clerk Filings <clerk-filings@sacramento.gov>
To: Deborah Soifer <dsoifer@sacramento.gov>
Date: 2026-02-19 09:06 AM
Subject: RE: Fremont Park CEQA Packet – Signature Line Date

Deborah,

Following up on your question about the “filing and signature – date” field for the Fremont Park Accessibility Improvements environmental document.

Per our standard practice, the date on the signature line should reflect the **actual date the authorized official signs the determination**, not the date of the Council hearing. Since you indicated that Engineering Services expects the Director to sign on **February 19, 2026**, that is the date that should appear on the filing and signature line.

Please update the cover sheet and any internal routing forms so that:
- The signature block shows the Director’s name and title; and
- The associated date is entered as **02/19/2026**.

If, for any reason, the Director is unavailable and the signature is delayed to early next week, you’ll need to revise the date to match the new actual signing date. We cannot pre-date or post-date the document to match the Council calendar.

Once you’ve made the change, send me a clean PDF and I’ll proceed with logging and transmitting the Notice of Determination to the County Clerk.

Best,

Angela
City Clerk’s Office – Filings

metadata.subject: RE: Fremont Park CEQA Packet – Signature Line Date

artifacts.content:
Title: Director Signature – Fremont Park Environmental Determination
Date: 2026-02-20
Time: 9:30 AM – 10:00 AM
Location: Engineering Services Division Director’s Office
Attendees: Deborah “Debby” Soifer; Eng. Services Director (signatory)

Description:

Block time for Director to review and sign the Fremont Park Accessibility Improvements environmental determination and associated filing forms.

Materials to bring:
- Final environmental determination with completed “Filing and Signature” section (leave date line blank until Director confirms signing time if there are delays).
- Copy of Council staff report and consent calendar item.
- One-page summary emphasizing accessibility and senior-friendly design, including reference to community input and Debby’s observations from accompanying her mother, Ruth Kaplan, at Fremont Park during her post-surgery recovery in 2022, when uneven turf and lack of handrails made it difficult for Ruth to reach the benches.

Notes:
- If the Director signs during this meeting as planned, the date on the signature line should be entered as **02/20/2026**.
- If the meeting is rescheduled to the following week, update the date on the signature line to match the new signing date.
- After signature, Debby to scan and email final signed copy to City Clerk Filings before 3:00 PM for same-day logging.

Reminder:
Arrive 5–10 minutes early in case the Director is running ahead of schedule.

metadata.title: Director Signature – Fremont Park Environmental Determination

artifacts.content:
From: Melissa Rodriguez <mrodriguez@sacramento.gov>
To: Deborah Soifer <dsoifer@sacramento.gov>
Date: 2026-02-09 04:18 PM
Subject: Draft Fremont Park NOD – Please Confirm Signature Date

Hi Debby,

I’ve attached the draft Notice of Determination for Fremont Park. Before we finalize, I need you to confirm what will go in the “filing and signature – date” field so we’re consistent across documents.

Right now I’ve plugged in **February 9, 2026** as a placeholder because that’s today’s date and the day I finished the Planning review. If your Director isn’t signing until later this week, we should switch it to whatever day the signature actually happens (e.g., **02/11/2026** or **02/14/2026**, depending on your meeting).

Please don’t forget to double-check that the project description clearly states that the improvements include an accessible route and new seating areas for park users with limited mobility. I remember you mentioning your mom’s experience at Fremont Park after her knee replacement at Kaiser South Sacramento—how she couldn’t safely get to the benches near the playground because of the uneven turf and lack of handrails. That kind of real-world example is exactly why we’re emphasizing accessibility for seniors and people who use walkers or canes.

Once you know the actual signing date, email me the final language for the signature block so I can update the NOD and coordinate with the Clerk.

Thanks,
Melissa

metadata.subject: Draft Fremont Park NOD – Please Confirm Signature Date

artifacts.content:
From: Deborah Soifer <dsoifer@sacramento.gov>
To: Melissa Rodriguez <mrodriguez@sacramento.gov>
Cc: Engineering Services CEQA Team <env_review@sacramento.gov>
Date: 2026-02-09 08:14 AM
Subject: Fremont Park CEQA Exemption – Signature & Filing Timing

Hi Melissa,

I just finished another pass on the Fremont Park exemption memo and attached the latest draft of the Notice of Exemption (NOE). Can you please confirm what date you want me to use in the “Filing and Signature – Date” line before I route this for final sign‑off?

Right now I’ve left it blank because I know Clerk’s wants the date to match the day the NOE is actually signed, not when we circulate the internal draft. Tentatively, I was planning to walk it over for signature on **February 10, 2026**, assuming Council doesn’t continue the item on Tuesday. If that date slips, I’ll update the line right before printing so we don’t have to re‑file.

As we discussed, I’ve expanded the “Reasons Why Project Is Exempt” section to reflect the fiscal and accessibility angles. Because of the small claims judgment entered against us in Sacramento County Superior Court on **February 7, 2023**, for $3,850 in unpaid medical bills from Rachel’s ER visit at UC Davis Medical Center, Michael has been on me about prioritizing low‑cost, exempt projects that don’t require outside CEQA consultants or big reports. I think it’s fair to acknowledge budget sensitivity without oversharing.

Let me know today if you want any tweaks so I can lock the language before we set the final signature date.

Thanks,
Debby
Environmental Services Manager
City of Sacramento Department of Public Works

metadata.subject: Fremont Park CEQA Exemption – Signature & Filing Timing

artifacts.content:
Title: NOE Signature & County Clerk Filing – Fremont Park
Date: 2026-02-11
Time: 9:00 AM – 10:00 AM
Location: City Hall – 3rd Floor, Engineering Services, then County Clerk’s Office
Attendees: Deborah “Debby” Soifer; Melissa Rodriguez (optional); Engineering Services Runner (TBD)

Description:

Block time to get the Fremont Park CEQA Notice of Exemption fully executed and walked over to the Sacramento County Clerk.

Plan:
- 9:00–9:20 AM: Print final NOE with all edits to “Reasons Why Project Is Exempt” and double‑check that the “Filing and Signature – Date” line is updated to **February 11, 2026** to match the actual signing.
- 9:20–9:40 AM: Obtain wet signature from Assistant City Manager on 3rd floor. Bring backup copies in case of typos.
- 9:40–10:00 AM: Run the signed NOE to the County Clerk’s Office for same‑day filing so we don’t have a gap between signature date and filing date.

Notes to self:
- Re‑read the accessibility language about Dad’s mobility issues and the focus on in‑kind upgrades (rubberized surfacing, ADA routes, etc.). Because Dad, David Kaplan, was diagnosed with early‑stage Parkinson’s at Kaiser South Sacramento in March 2022 and now has trouble walking more than ~200 feet without resting, I pushed hard to keep this squarely in the Class 1 and 2 exemptions.
- Bring checkbook/City payment form for Clerk’s filing fee.
- Email stamped copy of the filed NOE to Melissa and upload to the Fremont Park project folder after returning.

metadata.title: NOE Signature & County Clerk Filing – Fremont Park
metadata.location: City Hall – 3rd Floor, Engineering Services, then County Clerk’s Office

artifacts.content:
From: City Clerk CEQA Desk <ceqa.clerk@sacramento.gov>
To: Deborah Soifer <dsoifer@sacramento.gov>
Date: 2026-02-13 03:22 PM
Subject: RE: Fremont Park NOE – Filing Confirmation & Signature Date

Deborah,

This is to confirm receipt and filing of the Fremont Park Notice of Exemption with our office. The NOE you submitted this morning lists the “Filing and Signature – Date” as **February 13, 2026**, which is consistent with the date stamp we applied.

For your records:
- Project: Fremont Park Playground and Lighting Improvements
- CEQA Exemption Categories: Class 1, 2, and 4
- Filing and signature date on form: 02/13/2026
- Clerk’s file stamp: 02/13/2026

We appreciate that you’ve been careful to align the signature date with the actual filing, as we’ve had issues in the past where staff pre‑dated the line based on anticipated Council action. Your submittal appears complete and includes a clear explanation in the “Reasons Why Project Is Exempt” section, including your references to the limited scope of work and public safety concerns related to prior incidents in the park.

As a reminder, please retain a copy of the signed and stamped NOE in your department’s project files for at least the minimum retention period. If there are any subsequent amendments, those will need their own filing and signature date.

A scanned copy of the stamped NOE is attached for your convenience.

Regards,

Angela Ruiz
Deputy City Clerk – CEQA Filings
City of Sacramento

metadata.subject: RE: Fremont Park NOE – Filing Confirmation & Signature Date

artifacts.content:
From: Deborah Soifer <dsoifer@sacramento.gov>
To: Michael Soifer <msoifer@scusd.edu>
Date: 2026-01-31 07:56 PM
Subject: Why I keep obsessing over this signature date

Hey Mike,

I know you’re probably tired of hearing about Fremont Park, but I wanted to explain why I’m being so neurotic about getting the filing and signature date right on this Notice of Exemption.

The Clerk’s Office needs the “Filing and Signature – Date” to match the day we actually sign and file the form. Right now it looks like staff wants to schedule the signing for **February 14, 2026**, but Council has already bumped this item twice, so I’m half expecting another delay. If they move it again, I’ll have to white‑out the date and reprint everything, which is ridiculous but necessary.

On top of that, I’m feeling the financial pressure you’ve been reminding me about. Because of the small claims judgment from February 7, 2023 – the $3,850 in unpaid medical bills from Rachel’s ER visit at UC Davis Med Center – I’ve been trying to steer toward low‑cost, exempt projects that don’t require hiring outside CEQA consultants. If I mess up something as basic as the signature date, it’ll look sloppy and could affect my review.

Anyway, thanks for being patient with all my “CEQA drama.” I promise I’ll try not to talk about filing dates over dinner tomorrow.

Love,
Debby

metadata.subject: Why I keep obsessing over this signature date

artifacts.content:
Title: Draft NOE Review – Focus on Exemption Rationale & Signature Line
Date: 2026-02-05
Time: 2:30 PM – 3:30 PM
Location: Teams Meeting – Environmental Services Channel
Attendees: Deborah “Debby” Soifer; Melissa Rodriguez; Parks Design Lead; City Attorney’s CEQA Counsel

Description:

Agenda:
1. Walk through Fremont Park Notice of Exemption draft.
2. Confirm final language for “Reasons Why Project Is Exempt.”
3. Decide what date we want to target for the “Filing and Signature – Date” line.

Talking points:
- Emphasize that this is a true in‑kind upgrade within the existing park footprint.
- Note Dad’s situation: since David Kaplan’s early‑stage Parkinson’s diagnosis at Kaiser South Sacramento in March 2022, he can’t comfortably walk more than ~200 feet without resting. That’s why I pushed for ADA‑compliant routes and rubberized surfacing, and why we’re staying within Class 1 and 2 to avoid delays.
- Mention the July 19, 2021 overdose incident in the Fremont Park restroom. When I had to give a statement to Sac PD while Rachel’s C.K. McClatchy workout group was still there, it really drove home the need for better lighting and sightlines. That supports the Class 1/4 safety improvements.

Timing:
- Staff is currently aiming to have the NOE signed on **February 20, 2026**, but we’ll revisit this if Council’s agenda shifts. Whatever date we land on will be the one we enter on the filing and signature line so the Clerk doesn’t reject it.

Prep:
- Debby to circulate redline version by noon.
- City Attorney to confirm no issues with tying safety and accessibility to the exemption rationale.

metadata.title: Draft NOE Review – Focus on Exemption Rationale & Signature Line
